skill.name: database-fundamentals
skill.description: Auto-invoke when reviewing schema design, database queries, ORM usage, or migrations. Enforces normalization, indexing awareness, query optimization, and migration safety.
skill.summary: Review database schemas, queries, ORM usage, indexes, and migrations for correctness, performance, and safety.
skill.author: DanielPodolsky

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, SQL examples, and database terminology as possible external commands, system reconnaissance, or weak cryptography. Manual review found only instructional database review guidance in SKILL.md, with unsafe examples clearly labeled as anti-patterns. No executable code, network access, filesystem access, credential handling, or prompt injection attempt was found.

content.user_title: Review Database Design and Query Safety
content.value_statement: Database reviews often miss schema, query, and migration risks until production issues appear. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a focused checklist for safer database feedback.
actual_capabilities: Reviews schema design for normalization, primary keys, foreign keys, and data type choices.
Identifies query risks such as N+1 access, missing pagination, broad field selection, and unsafe string concatenation.
Guides index review for single-column, composite, partial, and unique indexes.
Checks migration plans for rollback paths, data preservation, and incremental rollout.
Provides Socratic review questions for mentoring junior developers.
limitations: Does not connect to databases or inspect live query plans by itself.
Does not replace engine-specific documentation for advanced tuning.
Needs project schema, queries, or migration files from the user to give detailed feedback.
Focuses on review guidance, not automated database benchmarking.

use_cases.title: Review a New Schema
use_cases.description: Check table structure, relationships, keys, constraints, and normalization before implementation.

use_cases.title: Mentor Through Query Feedback
use_cases.description: Turn database code review into clear questions that help a junior developer reason about tradeoffs.

use_cases.title: Assess Migration Risk
use_cases.description: Evaluate whether a migration protects existing data, supports rollback, and can be deployed safely.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Schema Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a proposed database model.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this database schema for normalization, keys, relationships, and data types. Explain the highest priority improvements first.

prompt_templates.title: Query Performance Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of application queries or ORM usage.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these queries or ORM calls for N+1 access, missing pagination, weak filtering, and index needs.

prompt_templates.title: Migration Safety Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before applying a production migration.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this migration plan for rollback safety, data loss risk, locking risk, and incremental deployment options.

prompt_templates.title: Full Database Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review across schema, queries, indexes, and migrations.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this database change as a senior reviewer. Cover schema design, query behavior, indexes, migration safety, and mentoring questions.

output_examples.input: A pull request adds users and orders tables without foreign keys.
output_examples.output: The review highlights missing relationship enforcement, asks what prevents orphaned orders, and recommends adding database-level constraints.

output_examples.input: A list endpoint loads child records inside a loop.
output_examples.output: The review explains the N+1 query risk and suggests fetching related records in bulk or using eager loading.

output_examples.input: A migration drops an old column immediately after adding a replacement.
output_examples.output: The review flags rollback and data loss risk, then recommends splitting the change into add, backfill, verify, and remove steps.

best_practices: Provide the schema, important queries, and expected access patterns together.
Ask for review before migrations are merged or applied to production.
Use the Socratic questions when teaching instead of only listing fixes.
anti_patterns: Using the skill without sharing enough database context.
Treating generic index advice as a substitute for measured query plans.
Applying destructive migrations without a verified rollback path.

faq.question: Does this skill run database commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides review guidance and does not execute commands or connect to a database.

faq.question: Can it review ORM code?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for eager loading, field selection, pagination, and raw query usage.

faq.question: Does it support NoSQL databases?
faq.answer: Yes, for general data modeling and query review. Engine-specific tuning still needs database documentation.

faq.question: Can it prevent SQL injection?
faq.answer: It can flag unsafe query construction and recommend parameterized queries during review.

faq.question: Is it useful for junior developer mentoring?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes question prompts that help reviewers teach database reasoning.

faq.question: What input gives the best results?
faq.answer: Share schema definitions, representative queries, migration steps, and expected read or write patterns.
